steps.title: Spot the emotional contrast
steps.content: The passage describes robots designed with features that help create a “comforting semblance of humanity.” But then signals a reversal: when robots look too human, people feel “more unsettled than” ____. The blank represents the intended positive state that the design aims for.

steps.title: Match to the designed emotional baseline
steps.content: Soothed matches the “comforting” emotional baseline the robots are designed to create. When robots appear too human-like, people feel more unsettled than soothed—directly undermining the design intent. The other choices—indifferent, revolted, and vindicated—are either emotionally opposed to the goal or unrelated to appearance-based reactions. That directly captures what the passage illustrates about excessive human-likeness in robot design. That matches choice A — A is correct.
